{{Quote|Another near legendary creature.|[[Lyrre T'enna]]'s notes on the Titavian|<ref name="WSW" />}}
The '''titavian''', also known as the '''goffbird''' or '''huge goff''',<ref name="DB-kaadu">{{DB|creature|kaadu|kaadu}}</ref> was a gigantic, near-legendary, [[Reptavian/Legends|reptavian]] native to [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="WSW" />

==Characteristics==
[[File:Titavian-woswfg.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Titavian wingspan]]
Goffs were huge flying reptavians that perched on mountains and crags at the remote edges of oceans and [[Gungan Swamp|swamps]] on Naboo. Large animal bones and limbs found near their perches suggested that the goffs ate carrion.<ref name="WSW" /> They had also been observed feeding on the floating carcasses of [[Sando aqua monster/Legends|sando aqua monsters]]. They were assumed to lay eggs. Their wingspan averaged 100 meters.<ref name="WSW">''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]''</ref>

[[File:Titavian2-woswfg.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A titavian wing shown with [[Aiwha/Legends|aiwhas]] for scale]]

They had difficulty taking off due to their large size. They needed a great deal of room to take off, so high cliffs were handy. They could spread their wings and leap off, then eventually catch the currents. They were vulnerable on flat ground, but they had no predators due to their massive size.<ref name="WSW" />

As a rite of passage, a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] warrior had to capture feathers from the wing of a titavian while riding an [[Aiwha/Legends|aiwha]].<ref name="WSW" /> Captured feathers were then used in their [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]] war saddles.<ref name="Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide">[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']]</ref> This was dangerous because, although titavians were not aggressive, one absent swipe of its massive wing could easily kill both rider and his aiwha mount.<ref name="WSW" />
